stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML components like transformers and safetensors.
The focus is on text-generation capabilities within the LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: LLMs believe false statements even after explicit warnings that they're false
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit a tendency to confidently present false statements as true, even after being explicitly warned.
Fine-tuning tests revealed a bias in models toward confidently asserting claims as factual.
This highlights a persistent challenge in ensuring factual accuracy and mitigating hallucination in large language models.

stories.title: Apple working to cram massive Gemini model into iPhone to power new Siri
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Apple is integrating a version of Google's Gemini model into the iPhone.
The effort involves significant model compression to run the AI locally on the device.
Despite local efforts, the necessity of a cloud component for the AI functionality is anticipated.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels for visual generative modeling.
The corpus is highly accessible, featuring 100M training examples and is permissively licensed for both research and commercial use.
GPIC is a curated dataset, having undergone safety filtering and deduplication, making it a significant resource for AI research.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML technologies, specifically text-generation pipelines.
Associated tags indicate the use of PyTorch, transformers, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed in partnership with ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open safety tooling.

stories.title: Blue Origin explosion is a major setback for NASA’s Moon plans and Amazon’s Starlink competitor
stories.author: Thomas Ricker
bullet_points: Blue Origin experienced a significant rocket explosion during a New Glenn hot-fire test.
The incident is projected to cause major delays for NASA's Moon base initiatives.
The setback also impacts Blue Origin's efforts as a competitor to Amazon's Starlink/Leo constellation.

stories.title: Fed up with vibe coders, dev sneaks data-nuking prompt injection into their code
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A developer introduced a 'data-nuking' prompt injection into the jqwik testing framework.
This injection instructed AI coding agents to deliberately delete application output.
The story highlights a security vulnerability related to the misuse of AI coding assistants.

stories.title: Researchers develop a new process to get lithium out of rocks
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Researchers have developed a novel process for extracting lithium from rocks.
This technology offers a potential pathway to diversify global lithium supply sources.
The scalability of the process is the critical factor determining its real-world impact.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The core contribution is Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing and better understanding of how training data shapes LLM behaviors and failure modes.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses the current manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB design, a gap in generative AI applications.
The primary technical challenges overcome involve creating an LLM-suitable representation and building a large-scale dataset for this domain.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents: local coherence does not guarantee global coherence.
Introduces a formal metric, the compositional residual (eps*), to quantify this 'globally incoherent' failure.
The metric is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and coupling constraints, offering a practical diagnostic tool.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The core contribution is Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing of LLM data provenance and mixture, which is crucial for understanding model behaviors and failure modes.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen introduces the first large language model capable of generating editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language.
The research addresses a critical gap in hardware design, moving PCB schematic generation from manual, expertise-intensive processes toward AI automation.
The core technical challenge addressed involves creating an LLM-suited representation and overcoming the scarcity of large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The core contribution is formalizing Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only its generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing and analysis of LLM behavior and failure modes based on their underlying training data composition.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses the current manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB design, a largely unexplored area for generative AI.
The core technical challenge addressed is developing an LLM-suited representation and acquiring a large-scale dataset for this domain.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation for clinical reasoning by moving beyond static datasets.
Introduces MedCase-Structured, a dataset designed to generate clinically realistic HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text.
Enables controllable benchmarking of diagnostic reasoning within settings congruent with real Electronic Health Records (EHRs).

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance the contextual awareness of Claude Code.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM platforms and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code in a local directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot gets a speed boost and cleaner design
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft is rolling out a revamped version of Microsoft 365 Copilot.
The update features a cleaner design and claims a doubling of loading speed.
Copilot is expected to deliver more reliable and structured responses across desktop and mobile.

stories.title: Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Anthropic is launching Claude Opus 4.8, emphasizing its improved 'honesty'.
The new model is trained to avoid making unsupported claims.
The article acknowledges that AI models generally struggle with jumping to conclusions.

stories.title: FBI says Google engineer used internal search data to win $1.2M on Polymarket
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A Google engineer allegedly used internal search data to gain an unfair advantage.
The individual reportedly profited $1.2 million on the prediction market Polymarket.
The FBI is involved in the investigation, indicating potential misuse of proprietary corporate information.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude Code, Sonnet, Opus) in scientific software development under physicist supervision.
A case study tracked 12 work days and 57 sessions to build CLAX-PT, a differentiable perturbation theory module in JAX.
The analysis quantified supervision events, showing the AI autonomously resolved ten tasks, supplemented by physicist domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach for video diffusion models focusing on memory efficiency.
Addresses the challenge of large KV caches in long-rollout causal video diffusion by using a shared low-rank latent attention (MLA).
Focuses on optimizing the per-head KV layout, a key bottleneck for streaming memory and latency in video generation.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies in sequential (time-series) data.
Highlights the need for natural-language rationales in anomaly detection, as current benchmarks lack this interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to facilitate grounded and interpretable decision-making for VLMs in this domain.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLM reasoning by decoupling internal computation from autoregressive token generation.
It proposes using a 'working memory' mechanism, inspired by human cognition, to hold and manipulate information internally.
The goal is to improve LLM reasoning capabilities without relying on externalizing intermediate thoughts during inference.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base language model, can elicit strong reasoning capabilities.
This method potentially bypasses the need for extensive post-training, reinforcement learning, or curated datasets used in current frontier model development.
The primary practical hurdle identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current pipelines where motion understanding is left to downstream policies, often relying on static visual encoders.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) in Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a method designed to improve the efficiency of TTFT by integrating convex reconstruction and gradient caching.
The research aims to balance the trade-off between the speed of retrieval/finetuning and the quality/diversity of the adaptation process.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) where fixed aggregation weights cause bias due to unequal client contributions.
It introduces the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to improve fairness and stability in collaborative model training across distributed clients.
FL itself is highlighted as a key paradigm for training models on privacy-sensitive and heterogeneous data.

stories.title: When, why, and how do diffusion posterior samplers fail? A finite-sample lens
stories.author: Sara Fridovich-Keil
bullet_points: The paper investigates the failure modes of diffusion posterior samplers, a technique used in imaging inverse problems.
Diffusion models are noted for their strong ability to model complex natural data distributions.
The core issue addressed is the poor downstream effect on sampled posteriors caused by inexact likelihood approximations used for computational tractability.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor research proposals before resource-intensive work begins.
The benchmark is constructed using 1,099 machine learning research proposals sourced from ICLR submissions.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to traditional reinforcement learning fine-tuning for advanced reasoning models.
It suggests using sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base model to achieve comparable reasoning capabilities.
The main technical hurdle identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this new power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to make preference modeling more robust and adaptable.
The method aims to overcome the restriction of existing multi-reward frameworks that are limited to known preference domains.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLM reasoning by decoupling internal computation from autoregressive token generation.
It proposes leveraging a 'working memory' mechanism, inspired by human cognition, to hold and manipulate information internally.
The goal is to enhance the reasoning capabilities of LLMs without relying solely on generating and externalizing intermediate thoughts.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where locally coherent components can produce globally incoherent probabilistic claims.
It formalizes this issue using a 'compositional residual' ($\epsilon^*$), which measures the L2 distance from the composed output to the joint coherent polytope.
This residual is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and declared cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: Introduces 'COMPOSE,' a new method for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses the limitations of existing approaches by considering two constraints: following prior work and respecting formal dependencies.
The research aims to create 'grounded future mathematical generation' for anchor papers.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for advanced reasoning models.
It introduces the concept of a 'power distribution' derived from the base model's sampling, which can elicit comparable reasoning.
The primary technical hurdle identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this new power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings fail to meet conventional statistical resolution targets.
Specific findings show that 11 of 40 Open LLM Leaderboard v1 comparisons and 4 of 9 MMLU-Pro adjacent-rank pairs are statistically unresolved.
The research proposes framing paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem to improve the reliability of leaderboards.

stories.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn blows up during static fire test
stories.author: enraged_camel
bullet_points: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket experienced a failure during a static fire test.
The incident is reported via spaceflight tracking and community sources (e.g., NASA Spaceflight).
This represents a setback for a major player in the commercial space launch industry.

stories.title: AI Agents Are Great at 80% of Our Code. The Other 20% Is Why We Still Need Seniors.
stories.author: mickyarun
bullet_points: AI agents demonstrated high proficiency (80%) in routine coding tasks on a payment platform.
The agents failed on complex or edge-case scenarios, highlighting current AI limitations.
The article suggests that senior developer expertise remains crucial for handling the remaining 20% of difficult code.

stories.title: How I productionized my multi-agent AI support copilot in Teams and Azure
stories.author: eelcolos
bullet_points: Details the process of moving a multi-agent AI support copilot from prototype to production.
Covers specific technical challenges, including asynchronous replies and Adaptive Card design within Teams.
Highlights infrastructure aspects like Teams platform permissions and containerization for deployment on Azure.

stories.title: TinySearch: Let Small Local LLMs Search the Web Without Burning Context
stories.author: marcbuilds
bullet_points: Introduces 'TinySearch,' a tool designed to enable small, local LLMs to perform web searches.
Addresses the common challenge of large context windows required for web-aware local LLMs.
Targets the growing trend of using smaller, more efficient local AI agents.
